title: Reverse Proxies, Forwarded Trust, Message Framing, and Smuggling Defense · Runtime Contract: Reverseproxy Rewrite Director
summary: A gateway preserves client Forwarded fields, uses deprecated ReverseProxy.Director, disagrees with an upstream about malformed framing, forwards hop-by-hop fields, and constructs unsafe backend queries. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo reverseproxy rewrite director increment in proxy rewrite and parser-consistency threat model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use ReverseProxy.Rewrite with separate inbound and outbound requests, SetURL and reviewed SetXForwarded behavior, and reject deprecated Director trust assumptions.
Trace downstream and upstream connections, hop-by-hop versus end-to-end fields, termination points, authority, scheme, client address, and failure ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Reverseproxy Rewrite Director · Theory Reverseproxy Rewrite Director
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 7894 in a canary service revision draining long-lived streams. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject ambiguous framing and deprecated Director-style proxy mutation; then replay the same operation key with different content and reject the mismatch. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
